purpose: The commit fixes a bug where commands could be queued after HCI_UNREGISTER was set, leading to use-after-free issues. It modifies hci_cmd_sync_queue to return an error in such cases.
Removes unnecessary asm/termios.h include in n_hdlc.c to clean up dependencies.
Updates documentation to reflect the addition of 'N/A' store metric in perf c2c tool.
Fixes race conditions and incorrect vma lock handling during split and unmapping. Ensures proper allocation and cleanup of locks to prevent issues.
Adds AVDD and MICVDD supply documentation to Realtek rt5682s codec device tree bindings.
The commit fixes a resource leak by adding iounmap() in remove function.
Removes an unused 'work' field from mlx5e_async_ctx struct in kTLS code.
Changes cfg80211_auth_request::key_idx to signed to prevent static checker warnings when -1 is assigned.
The commit sorts CONFIG_SURFACE_PLATFORMS in defconfigs for RISC-V, with no functional changes.
Refactors code by replacing an open-coded check with a helper function to determine CAN-FD mode.
Modifies self-tests to skip RTC tests when the hardware is not present, preventing test failures.
Removes redundant assignment of variable 'bytes' which was causing a compiler warning about dead code.
Decouples rcuscale from RCU Tasks to reduce kernel size and tracing complexity.
The commit removes a duplicated phrase in a comment within spte.h, improving code clarity.
Reverts populating DCN315 clock table based on DCFCLK due to underflow issues when states are disabled.
Adds compatible string for Renesas RZ/V2L SoC with Mali-G31 GPU in device tree bindings.
Renames struct pcie_port to dw_pcie_rp for naming consistency in the DW PCIe core driver.
Fixes clang -Wformat warning by correcting format specifier from %hx to %x due to integer promotion of u16 to int.
The commit fixes a warning in the MIPS jump_label by casting the branch range upper bound to long for correct signed comparison.
Removes HW_VAR_H2C_FW_P2P_PS_OFFLOAD case from SetHwReg8188EU(), replacing it with direct function call to simplify code as part of removing unwanted HAL layer.
The commit changes non-global symbols to static in arm64 code to fix sparse warnings, improving code quality.
Removes incorrect interrupt flags from MDSS nodes in device tree bindings to correct configuration.
Allocates arrays in struct ctrl_ref to allow future driver changes.
Adds support for additional PMICs and SoCs in the socinfo driver.
The commit enhances the WiFi driver to recognize all possible chip cuts (versions A-P), improving identification beyond the previous limit of E.
Removes custom enable count for PRCMU clocks as core already tracks it.
Refactors RISC-V mm init code to reduce #ifdef CONFIG_XIP_KERNEL usage by grouping similar cases, improving code readability and maintainability.
Enables QUP and GPI DMA hardware to support future I2C functionality.
The commit improves code comments by clarifying descriptions, fixing a typo, and correcting coding style.
Fixes an infinite loop caused by failed HW semaphore acquisition during hardware removal, adding error logging.
The commit updates the pcm3168a driver to use modern ASoC DAI clocking terminology, improving code readability and maintainability.
Removes the IS_SWAPFILE hack as it's always defined, making the code cleaner.
Implements GTP echo response to comply with specifications by sending GTP_ECHO_RSP with recovery info.
Replaces cpumask_next_* functions with cpumask_first_* for efficiency and adds cpumask_first_zero().
Removes an obsolete firmware call causing console errors during roaming.
Adds board config adl_rt5682 for alc5682 codec without amplifier, connecting to SSP0.
The commit modifies io_uring/net to fail zerocopy sendmsg when the socket doesn't support it, preventing potential bugs.
The commit modifies the kernel documentation build process to test for xeCJK.sty before loading it, preventing xelatex errors on systems where texlive-xecjk is not properly packaged.
The commit modifies the checkpatch script to allow certain macros without triggering errors, improving code checking accuracy.
Refactor simpledrm to use atomic helpers instead of simple-KMS helpers. No functional changes; improves code architecture for better maintainability.
Adds jpeg decode worker interface for parallel HW processing on MT8195.
Removes VPRINTK() calls and ata_scsi_dump_cdb(), replacing them with tracepoint-based debugging to improve code maintainability.
Adds support for entering zstate during extended vblank periods in AMD display driver, optimizing power usage by calculating gained time and setting optimized values.
Fixes a CFI failure caused by incorrect function parameter type.
Corrects 'resets' property name in STM32 pinctrl bindings and adds missing 'additionalProperties: false'.
Silences an array-bounds warning in ath9k driver by adjusting array indexing.
Replaces pm_runtime_get_sync with pm_runtime_resume_and_get in sun8i-ss crypto driver to simplify code.
Refactors io_uring by splitting fixed file handling into the filetable code area and modifies helper functions to use context instead of io_kiocb.
The commit updates device tree bindings to split G1 and G2 decoder nodes for better power control support.
Removes the IWL_DEVICE_BZ_COMMON macro as it's redundant and only used once within another macro.
